assertions.title: Help shows canonical ref-based review usage

steps.action: Run the CLI help surface with `review --help`.
steps.expected: Exit 0 and advertise only `--repo`, `--base`, and `--head` review inputs.
steps.observed: Usage:   mix cerberus.review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]   bin/cerberus review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]

assertions.title: Review a local repository by refs from any working directory

steps.action: Run a ref-range review from the separate caller repo using `--repo <target>`.
steps.expected: The CLI should succeed from outside the target repo and print a human-readable verdict and summary.
steps.observed: Verdict: PASS Summary: All reviewers passed.  Findings: - none

steps.action: Inspect resolved refs and tool-read content.
steps.expected: The run should resolve the requested target refs and read the head commit content, not the dirty target worktree.
steps.observed: Resolved 89179bfd215a16d453cb158e989aea8a664f11ca..70bdd788b4340fd0184df2938c836b273d70da2a; tool content matched head commit=True, differed from dirty worktree=True

assertions.title: Commit-ish refs use an isolated materialized workspace

steps.action: Run representative commit-ish pairs (revision expression→branch, tag, full SHA, short SHA) from the caller repo.
Compare caller git state before and after the runs.
steps.expected: Caller HEAD, status, and worktree list remain unchanged.
steps.observed: HEAD unchanged=True, status unchanged=True, worktrees unchanged=True

steps.action: Inspect tool-read content for the revision-expression case.
steps.expected: Workspace materialization should expose the committed head content rather than dirty target files.
steps.observed: Matched head commit=True, differed from dirty worktree=True

steps.action: Run a review where `--base` and `--head` resolve to the same commit.
steps.observed: Verdict: SKIP Summary: No changes to review between 70bdd788b4340fd0184df2938c836b273d70da2a (70bdd788b4340fd0184df2938c836b273d70da2a) and 70bdd788b4340fd0184df2938c836b273d70da2a (70bdd788b4340fd0184df2938c836b273d70da2a).  Findings: -...

steps.action: Check planner/reviewer marker files and workspace temp entries.
steps.expected: No planner/reviewer work and no review workspace materialization.
steps.observed: reviewer marker=False, router marker=False, workspace entries=[]

steps.action: Run missing-flags, unknown-flag, legacy `--diff`, nonexistent `--repo`, and non-git-directory invocations.
steps.expected: Each case should fail non-zero with actionable diagnostics.
steps.observed: missing-flags=>exit 1 (Missing required options: --repo, --base, --head.  Usage:   mix cerberus.review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]   bin/cerberus review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]); unknown-flag=>exit 1 (Unsupported options: ----unknown  Usage:   mix cerberus.review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]   bin/cerberus review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]); legacy-diff=>exit 1 (Legacy --diff input is no longer supported. Use --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref>.  Usage:   mix cerberus.review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <ref> [--format json|text]   bin/cerberus review --repo <path> --base <ref> --head <...); missing-repo=>exit 1 (Repository path not found for --repo: /definitely/missing/repo); non-git-dir=>exit 1 (--repo is not inside a Git repository: /var/folders/jr/0kj0xfdd4s1ggs921sr2d7f80000gn/T/utv_cli_contract_71457f7b_jm_vb3dq/utv_cli_contract_71457f7b_plain_dir)

steps.action: Inspect marker files and temp workspace prefixes for each invalid case.
steps.observed: missing-flags: reviewer=False, router=False, workspaces=[]; unknown-flag: reviewer=False, router=False, workspaces=[]; legacy-diff: reviewer=False, router=False, workspaces=[]; missing-repo: reviewer=False, router=False, workspaces=[]; non-git-dir: reviewer=False, router=False, workspaces=[]

steps.action: Run a review with an unresolvable `--head` ref.
steps.expected: The CLI should fail non-zero and name the bad ref.
steps.observed: Could not resolve --head ref "missing-ref" inside /private/var/folders/jr/0kj0xfdd4s1ggs921sr2d7f80000gn/T/utv_cli_contract_71457f7b_jm_vb3dq/utv_cli_contract_71457f7b_target_repo: fatal: Needed a single revision

steps.expected: No workspace, planner, or reviewer work should start.
steps.observed: reviewer marker=False, router marker=False, workspace entries=[]

steps.action: Run a successful local review with GitHub/server bootstrap variables removed from the environment.
steps.observed: Verdict: PASS Summary: All reviewers passed.  Findings: - none

steps.action: Compare process/port snapshots before and after the run.
steps.expected: No Cerberus listener or new interesting port binding should remain.
steps.observed: new interesting listener ports=[], new cerberus processes=[]

assertions.title: Completed review exit statuses are deterministic

steps.action: Run representative PASS, WARN, and SKIP completions.
Run representative FAIL, invocation-failure, and runtime-failure cases.
steps.expected: FAIL exits a distinct non-zero code from invocation/runtime failures.

frictions.description: Deterministic CLI validation required a temporary `mix run` wrapper script so the supported `:cli_overrides` runtime surface could inject anonymous-function doubles and unique supervisor names per run.
frictions.resolution: Executed every assertion through `mix run --no-start --no-compile <temp-runner>.exs -- review ...` with isolated temp repos and per-run names, without modifying repository source files.
